gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف بسندي ه - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/152)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف بسندي ه - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/152)
text: ذَكَرَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) الْبَيْضَ فَقَالَ أَمَا إِنَّهُ خَفِيفٌ يَذْهَبُ بِقَرَمِ اللَّحْمِ .
قَالَ وَ رَوَاهُ مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ إِسْمَاعِيلَ بْنِ بَزِيعٍ عَنْ جَعْفَرِ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ حَكِيمٍ عَنْ مُرَازِمٍ أَنَّهُ زَادَ فِيهِ وَ لَيْسَتْ لَهُ غَائِلَةُ اللَّحْمِ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Khalid, from Ja’far Bin Muhammad Bin Hakeym, from Yunus, from Murazam who said,
‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> mentioned the eggs, so he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘But, it is light, it does away with (reduces) nibbling the meat’.
He (the narrator) said, ‘An Muhammad Bin Ismail Bin Baie reported from Ja’far Bin Muhammad Bin Hakeym, from Murazam having increased in it,
(He<sup>asws</sup> said): ‘And it does not have for it the suffering pain of the meat’.
en.sarwar: 1. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Khalid from Ja‘far ibn Muhammad Hakim from Yunus from Murazim who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, once mentioned eggs and said, ‘It is light and it reduces the desire for meat.’ “He (the narrator) has said, ‘Muhammad ibn Tsma‘il ibn Bazi‘ has narrated from Ja‘far ibn Muhammad ibn Hakim from Murazim with an addition of, ‘it does not have the harmfulness of meat.”
